meaning: n.(要人的一批)随行人员,扈从
examples: Mind trainers are now part of a tennis star's retinue.
To feed and reward his huge retinue, he took 30,000 donkey-loads of grain from Kabul and Ghazni.
To feed and reward his huge retinue, he took 30, 000 donkey-loads of grain from Kabul and Ghazni.
Plenty there for Mr Cable and his new retinue of Tory junior ministers to tussle over.
The day I checked in, the King of Gambia was on his way out, along with a large retinue.
